error.signature: GitHub API returns 403 Abuse Detection for legitimate CI/CD automation

verdict.summary: GitHub's abuse detection triggers on patterns, not just rate: creating many PRs quickly, commenting on many issues, rapid content creation, or concurrent identical requests. Legitimate CI/CD tools are frequently flagged.

dead_ends.action: Stay under the documented 5000 req/hr rate limit
dead_ends.why_fails: Abuse detection is behavior-based, not rate-based. You can be flagged at 50 req/hr if the pattern looks automated (rapid sequential mutations).

dead_ends.action: Use a GitHub App instead of PAT to avoid abuse detection
dead_ends.why_fails: GitHub Apps have higher rate limits but are equally subject to abuse detection on mutation endpoints.

workarounds.action: Add 1-2 second delays between mutating API calls (create/update/delete)
workarounds.how: time.sleep(1)  # between each PR creation, comment, or issue update

workarounds.action: Use GraphQL for read-heavy workloads (single request for multiple resources)
workarounds.how: GraphQL queries count as single request. Fetching 100 PRs via REST = 100 requests, via GraphQL = 1.

workarounds.action: Implement exponential backoff with minimum 60s wait on 403
workarounds.how: On 403 abuse detection: wait 60s, then 120s, then 240s. Check Retry-After header if present.
